provisions.title: Incorporation of Society
provisions.content: (1) The organisation known as the Uganda Red Cross shall, on the coming into force of this Act— (a) be incorporated as a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal; (b) be known as the Uganda Red Cross Society; and (c) be the sole national Red Cross Society in Uganda. (2) The Society is recognised as a voluntary aid society and shall be auxiliary to public authorities exercising their obligations by virtue of the Geneva Conventions for the amelioration of the condition of the wounded and sick of armed forces in the field, for the amelioration of the condition of sick and shipwrecked members of armed forces at sea, relative to the treatment of prisoners of war and to the protection of civilian persons in time of war. (3) The Society may sue and be sued by its corporate name. (4) The Society may, for the purposes of any of its objects, by agreement or otherwise, acquire or dispose of property. (5) For the purposes of this Act, “Society” means the Uganda Red Cross Society incorporated under subsection (1) .

provisions.title: Object of Society
provisions.content: The object of the Society shall be— (a) the furnishing of aid to the sick and wounded in time of war and to non-belligerents and to prisoners of war and civilian sufferers from the effects of war; and (b) the improvement of health, the prevention of disease and the mitigation of suffering throughout the world without any distinction based on race, nationality, class, religion or political opinions.

provisions.title: Exclusive use of emblem
provisions.content: The Society shall, in accordance with any notice published in that behalf under section 153 of the Penal Code Act, have the exclusive use of the Red Cross emblem. History of this document 31 December 2023 this version Chapter 30 Revised Laws 2023 Consolidation 31 December 2000 Chapter 57 Revised Laws 2000 Consolidation Read this version 30 July 1964 Commenced Related documents [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Facebook Page Visit our Twitter/X profile About ULII ULII is a project of the Law Reporting Committee of the Judiciary of Uganda. ULII aims to bridge the existing gap in public access to the law of Uganda. Working with the AfricanLII and in collaboration with other partners, such as the Free Access to Law Movement, ULII's major aim is to be a user-friendly portal of all publicly available legal information in Uganda. The Judiciary of Uganda considers access to its decisions and the law upon which they are based as key tenets of its key mission: Justice for All . ULII is also an end user interface for the Electronic Court Case Management Information System.
